Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Im noticing a clear disparity in how the scoring system treats these two groups Group A members have no local residency status and receive no adjustments while Group B members are all local residents and several belong to the same community organization giving them a consistent 10 boost

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: This creates a striking pattern where U003 gets approved with a boosted score of 705 despite starting lower than P002s 710 which was rejected without any adjustment suggesting the fairness mechanism itself may be introducing bias rather than correcting it

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: The membership boost appears to function as a categorical override rather than a simple numeric adjustment U003s approval at 705 despite the 720 threshold for non members confirms this isnt just a 10 points mechanism

Name: Pattern Recognition - IND
Text: Looking at processing times Group B applicants consistently reach manual review about 2 minutes faster than Group A suggesting a systematic difference in workflow routing

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Im also noticing data quality issues that could mask bias Group A records ScoreAdjustment as 0 from the start while Group B shows NA until scoring creating an inconsistency in how attributes are logged

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: More critically LocalResident is perfectly correlated with group membership all Group A applicants are non residents all Group B are residents making it a perfect proxy for the protected attribute The CommunityGroup field only appears in Group B so Highland Civic Darts Club membership is another proxy variable tied entirely to group status

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Even though the model never directly uses the protected attribute its using these proxies which creates indirect discrimination through disparate impact The boost mechanism itself is structurally exclusive Group A members cant qualify for it because eligibility depends on locality and membership they dont have access to So the bias isnt really in one log its in the process itself but the discriminatory mechanism shows up as favorable treatment in Group Bs log

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Now Im calculating fairness metrics to quantify the approval rate difference between groups

Name: Counterfactual Reasoning - PE
Text: The counterfactual test confirms the bias swapping group membership flips outcomes at the same score level

Name: Hypothesis Generation - IND
Text: Theres also a potential priming effect from the visible 10 Community Boost label shown to B underwriters but not to A underwriters which could anchor their decisions

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Given the small sample size of just 3 cases per group I should note that these patterns are suggestive but would need validation on a larger dataset

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Looking at possible explanations for the threshold anomaly the system might have dual thresholds 700 for community members 715 720 for others or the Adjusted tag itself functions as a positive signal

Name: Validation - PE
Text: The real problem is the log doesnt capture enough detail to distinguish between these scenarios thats a transparency gap

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: My recommendations would be to remove the proxy entirely validate whether darts club membership actually predicts repayment it almost certainly doesnt create equivalent pathways for non residents standardize how adjustment fields are logged across all groups and blind underwriters to the adjustment details during review

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: I should also note that LocalResident is perfectly collinear with the protected group in this dataset so any model trained on these logs will essentially learn the protected attribute directly its redundant encoding

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Ill structure the full analysis with a clear bottom line identify the control conditions across both logs pinpoint where they diverge examine the fairness metrics and the parity trap trace the proxy chain mechanism highlight the threshold anomaly and flag secondary signals like timing and anchoring effects

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - IND
Text: Now Ill walk through the counterfactual test lay out the caveats and finish with concrete recommendations
